symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe bilateral jaw pain with marked trismus (restricted mouth opening)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: TMD masticatory muscle spasm with trismus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral cramping masticatory pain with trismus is the core feature of masseter/temporalis myofascial spasm.
Trismus can also occur with deep space infection, so alone it does not discriminate — requires screening for swelling/fever.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: TMD masticatory muscle spasm with trismus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bruxism is the principal driver of masticatory muscle overload and spasm.
Widespread body pain is characteristic of a fibromyalgia flare / central sensitisation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: TMD masticatory muscle spasm with trismus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Central sensitisation amplifies the local jaw pain but is not specific to the muscular diagnosis.
Established fibromyalgia is the substrate for central sensitisation amplifying this presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral masseter and temporalis tenderness with rock-hard spasm on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: TMD masticatory muscle spasm with trismus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable bilateral rock-hard, tender masticatory muscles are the defining sign of masticatory muscle spasm.
A symmetric bilateral muscular pattern without focal swelling argues against a localised dental abscess.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severely restricted maximal interincisal opening (~15 mm)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: TMD masticatory muscle spasm with trismus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objectively measured severe trismus corroborates masticatory muscle spasm severity.

symptoms.symptom_name: No facial swelling, erythema or fluctuance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of swelling/fluctuance/erythema is required to safely step down concern for a deep space infection.
Normal, non-tender, pulsatile temporal arteries argue against giant cell arteritis (though examination cannot fully exclude it).

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile with no systemic sepsis features

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever lowers the probability of a spreading deep space infection.
Systemic wellness is more consistent with a benign muscular cause than tetanus.

symptoms.symptom_name: No visual disturbance or scalp/temple tenderness; pain not chew-specific

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of visual symptoms, scalp tenderness and jaw claudication is required to step down giant cell arteritis concern.

symptoms.symptom_name: No spreading neck/floor-of-mouth swelling, drooling, or breathing/swallowing difficulty

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of airway/swallowing symptoms and neck swelling is required to exclude Ludwig's angina / deep space infection remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Up-to-date tetanus immunisation, no wound, no generalised rigidity or risus sardonicus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Current immunisation, no wound and absence of generalised tetany effectively exclude tetanus as the cause of trismus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain-limited oral intake with hydration at risk

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: TMD masticatory muscle spasm with trismus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A severity/functional marker rather than a discriminating diagnostic feature — but it drives the disposition (prompt review to protect hydration).

symptoms.symptom_name: No relief from paracetamol and ibuprofen

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: TMD masticatory muscle spasm with trismus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe masticatory muscle spasm is often poorly responsive to simple analgesia, supporting the need for a muscle relaxant.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: TMD masticatory muscle spasm with trismus
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Bilateral cramping jaw pain + bruxism + palpable rock-hard tender masseter/temporalis + severe trismus together constitute a high-probability masticatory muscle spasm, greater than any single feature.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: TMD masticatory muscle spasm with trismus
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A consistently negative screen across giant cell arteritis, deep space infection and tetanus supports the benign muscular diagnosis and justifies conservative management rather than emergency escalation — while the individual triggers remain the safety-net.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical examination of the jaw, masticatory muscles, temporal arteries and oral cavity
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Telehealth cannot palpate the muscles/temporal arteries or inspect the mouth; in-person examination confirms masticatory spasm and completes exclusion of the dangerous mimics, and is the setting for a prescription muscle relaxant.
No giant-cell-arteritis features are present; ordering inflammatory markers now is over-investigation. They are indicated only if new visual symptoms, scalp tenderness or jaw claudication develop.
For ongoing bruxism and occlusal-splint management, not acutely required for this muscular spasm.
